seo.descDefault: Learn to create effective AI prompts: modules, practice, quiz.
seo.descDefaultWithModules: Learn to create effective AI prompts: up to {{count}} modules, practice, quiz.
seo.descModules: Choose a training module: 6-block system, knowledge test, practice, context engineering, advanced test, project.
seo.descGlossary: Terminology: AI, prompt, RAG, token, etc.
seo.descTools: AI tools mentioned in the training: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.

home.title: Prompt anatomy
home.subline: 90% of people write prompts the wrong way – here you learn to do it right.
home.subtitle: Learn to create effective AI prompts with the
home.bonus: Practice for real business situations. ~45 min.
home.sectionTitle: Learn to create prompts that work.
home.sectionSubtitle: Through 6 practical blocks you'll build your own AI communication system.
home.feature3Desc: Create your prompts here and now.
home.feature4Desc: See your growth module by module.
home.howTo2: Paste into ChatGPT, Claude or another AI tool

module.notAccessibleHint: Choose a module from the list.
module.slideNavAria: Slide content. Swipe left – next slide, right – previous.
module.unlockCertBodyTier1: You completed 3 modules – you can download the Part 1 certificate.
module.unlockCertBodyTier2: You completed the course with ≥70% on the quiz – you can download the full certificate.
module.unlockCertBodyTier3: You completed the Data analytics path (modules 7–9) with ≥70% on the Module 8 test – you can download the advanced path certificate.
module.courseLinkAria: Course and more: promptanatomy.app (open in new tab)
module.useCaseM1_1: Project manager: use the same 6-block template for meeting agendas or reports – specify role (Meta), data (Input), and desired format (Output).
module.useCaseM1_2: Marketing: write campaign copy or plans – set the role (e.g. copywriter), add facts and constraints (Input), define the output format (Output).
module.useCaseM1_3: HR / people: prepare survey questions or job descriptions with a clear structure – role (Meta), list of questions or requirements (Input), format (Output).
module.useCaseM1_4: Analyst: phrase tasks for data analysis – what result you want (Output) and what data you provide (Input); AI will respond better when everything is clear.
module.useCaseM3_1: Project manager: reuse the module scenarios – quarterly report for the board or sales analysis with an action plan. Copy the template and adapt it to your numbers and deadlines.
module.useCaseM3_2: Marketing: campaign or product launch plan – use the scenario template: audience, channels, budget (Input), output format (Output).
module.useCaseM3_3: HR / people: employee survey analysis and action plan for leadership – plug your survey data into the same 6-block prompt and get a structured report.
module.useCaseM3_4: Analyst: sales or other data analysis with concrete actions – use the “numbers → insights → actions” scenario and replace the data (Input) for your case.
module.useCaseM6_1: Presentation brief and structure – reuse the same process for other decks.
module.useCaseM6_2: Sprint with AI – repeat brief → structure → slides for another topic or audience.
module.useCaseM6_3: Quality checklist – use the same checks before any presentation.
module.useCaseM6_4: Tool stack – Gamma, ChatGPT, Canva – adapt to other projects.

testPractice.projectOutcomeTitle: What you get after the project
testPractice.projectOutcomeDesc: Concrete outcomes – what you can use right away.
testPractice.selfAssessmentDesc: Before or after the project: tick what you applied.
testPractice.projectStepsDesc: You can stop and save after each step.
testPractice.projectStep1: Goal and role (META) – what you create, for whom, what role. You can stop here.
testPractice.projectStep2: Sources and constraints (INPUT + RAG) – what data you use. You can stop here.
testPractice.projectStep3: Format (OUTPUT) – structure, length, language. You can stop here.
testPractice.projectStep4: Research plan (REASONING / Deep research) – steps, sub-questions. You can stop here.
testPractice.projectStep5: Quality and checks (QUALITY) – sources, "I don't know", objectivity. You can stop here.
testPractice.projectStep6: Technical settings (ADVANCED) – temperature, max_tokens. When ready: run the prompt and get the report.
testPractice.outcomesRegionAria: What you'll learn in this module
testPractice.toolsCollapsibleAria: Expand or collapse the list of AI tools for analytics
testPractice.toolsPrincipleNote: Principles work across tools – prompt structure matters, not the platform.
testPractice.emptyPromptNote: Unclear goal. No context. AI guesses.
testPractice.pressCharacterHint: Click a character – their 4 tasks will open:
testPractice.m9WorkflowFirstFooter: First use Continue above – you will see the diagram and copy-paste prompts (2 slides). That is the core of this module.
testPractice.m9RecommendedAfterWorkflow: After the main path – recommended extra scenarios
testPractice.m9OptionalLightbulb: These scenarios are optional – use them for more topic-specific practice, or come back later.

testPractice.m5IntroTitleDefault: Mini test after the presentation sprint
testPractice.m5IntroBodyDefault: You just completed a **15‑min presentation sprint** (you created a presentation draft from existing information). This mini test checks if you're ready for Module 6 project: clear brief, structure, tool choice and quick quality check.
testPractice.m5ThresholdsTextDefault: Result **≥70%** – recommended to proceed to Module 6 (practice). Result **<70%** – recommended to retake the mini test and review "Structured process" (43) and "Presentation workflow" (47). You can still continue if you wish.
testPractice.passedMessageDefault: Result **{{score}}%**. You can proceed to Module 6 practice.
testPractice.failedMessageDefault: We recommend retaking the mini test first. If you still need clarity, review the slides indicated below. You can still continue to module completion if you wish.

testPractice.selectScenarioHintB: Choose a scenario below – recommended for beginners are shown first.

contentSlides.promptTypesHeroTitle: One prompt, three layers. Each one does a different job.
contentSlides.promptTypesHeroSubtitle: Combine them and the AI will understand not only WHAT, but also HOW and WHY.
contentSlides.promptTechniquesLogicTitle: Technique logic: from simple to advanced
contentSlides.instructGptChartAria: Line chart: quality score by model size
contentSlides.journeyConfirmMessage: You chose: {label}. Next in the module you will find templates tailored to this area.
contentSlides.nextStepIntro: Now that you've learned the 6-block system, workflow and techniques, it's time to check your knowledge – Module 2 test.
contentSlides.recommendedToolStatusAria: Recommended tool for this category marked with green check
contentSlides.promptTemplateIntro: 3 blocks. 1 template. Copy and use right away.
contentSlides.promptTemplateSub: META + INPUT + OUTPUT = a minimal but effective prompt foundation.
contentSlides.practiceSummaryDefaultSubtitle: Congratulations! You have successfully completed the Prompt Anatomy training and can now create professional, structured prompts.
contentSlides.practiceSummaryDefaultTaglineSub: 5 minutes for a good prompt = hours saved later
contentSlides.blockMetaQuestion: Question: Who are you and what do you do?
contentSlides.blockMetaBody: The Meta block sets the AI's identity and context. It's like a job description that determines how the AI interprets your task.
contentSlides.blockMetaGoodExample: You are a senior business analyst with 10 years of experience in e-commerce. Your goal is to prepare a sales report for the board, who will make strategic decisions for Q4.
contentSlides.blockMetaGoodExampleShort: You are a senior business analyst with 10 years of experience in e-commerce. Your goal is to prepare a sales report for the board, who will make strategic decisions for Q4.
contentSlides.blockMetaAudience: Target audience: who the result is for
contentSlides.blockInputQuestion: Question: What factual information, data, and constraints?
contentSlides.blockInputBody: The Input block specifies the CONCRETE data the AI should work with. Clear input = clear output.
contentSlides.blockInputWhatToInclude: What to include in the Input block?
contentSlides.blockInputGoodExample: Evaluate Q3 2024 sales. Data: 250k EUR revenue (+15% vs Q2), 1200 orders, average order value 208 EUR.
contentSlides.blockOutputQuestion: Question: What format and structure do I want?
contentSlides.blockOutputBody: The Output block specifies the EXACT result format. That removes unnecessary revision cycles.
contentSlides.blockOutputStructure2: Trends (comparison with Q3 and prior year Q4)
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsHero3: This isn't AI "natural thinking" – it's structure you specify in the prompt.
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsImgAlt: Reasoning models: Chain of Thought vs Tree of Thoughts
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsCotDesc: Step by step. One logical sequence.
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsTotDesc: Multiple branches. AI compares options and chooses.
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsWarning: If you don't specify reasoning structure – AI will choose it randomly or superficially.
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsCotExample:
REASONING (CoT):
1. Define Q3 sales metrics
2. Compare with Q2 and prior year Q3
3. Identify main trends
4. Formulate conclusion for Q4 forecast
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsTotExample:
REASONING (ToT):
1. State the problem: low LinkedIn engagement
2. Generate 3 solution options:
   a) Informational post
   b) Question format
   c) Provocative insight
3. Evaluate pros and cons of each
4. Choose the best for B2B audience
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsCotTemplate:
REASONING (CoT):
1. Define the problem
2. Analyse the data
3. Draw a conclusion
contentSlides.blockReasoningModelsTotTemplate:
REASONING (ToT):
1. State the problem
2. Generate [N] solution options
3. Evaluate pros and cons of each
4. Choose the best
contentSlides.blockReasoningHero1: Not every AI task needs reasoning. But every important one does.
contentSlides.blockReasoningHero2: Reasoning block: how to think before answering?
contentSlides.blockReasoningHero3: Specifies the AI's decision logic – not just text, but a justified decision.
contentSlides.blockReasoningUse1: you need a decision or recommendation
contentSlides.blockReasoningUse3: you need to weigh risks or trade-offs
contentSlides.blockReasoningDont2: you need to rewrite or shorten text
contentSlides.blockReasoningDont3: the answer is single and obvious
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep1Desc: What is the real problem to solve?
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep2Desc: What do we already know? Facts, constraints, context?
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep3Desc: What's missing for a full solution? What assumptions are made?
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep4Desc: What are the possible ways to solve it?
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep5Desc: Pros and cons of each option?
contentSlides.blockReasoningStep6Desc: Which solution to recommend and why?
contentSlides.blockReasoningWarning1: Reasoning won't work miracles if the problem is unclear, data is wrong, or the goal is undefined.
contentSlides.blockReasoningWarning2: Good reasoning starts with a clear problem.
contentSlides.blockReasoningExample1:
REASONING:
1. Problem: Employees use ChatGPT with no guidelines.
2. Data: 40 employees, sensitive data, no AI policy.
3. Missing: clear usage limits and accountability.
4. Options:
   a) Ban AI entirely
   b) Allow use with no limits
   c) Create an AI policy
5. Trade-offs:
   a) Safe but reduces efficiency
   b) Fast but risky
   c) Requires effort but manageable
6. Conclusion: Recommend creating an AI policy.
contentSlides.blockReasoningExample2:
REASONING:
1. Problem: LinkedIn posts get no engagement.
2. Data: B2B audience, low engagement.
3. Missing: clear CTA.
4. Options:
   a) Informational post
   b) Question format
   c) Provocative insight
5. Trade-offs:
   a) Safe but boring
   b) Encourages comments
   c) Risky but viral
6. Conclusion: Choose question format.
contentSlides.blockReasoningFullTemplate:
REASONING:
1. Problem:
2. Available data:
3. What's missing:
4. Possible options:
5. Trade-offs:
6. Conclusion:
contentSlides.blockQualityHero1: An AI response can look great. And be completely wrong.
contentSlides.blockQualityHero2: Quality Control: how to check the result?
contentSlides.blockQualityHero3: A safety belt that makes AI state assumptions and point out limitations.
contentSlides.blockQualityNotEqualBody: A good answer doesn't mean good reasoning. AI can give a polished but unfounded answer.
contentSlides.blockQualityBadDesc: Looks professional but has no logic or assumptions.
contentSlides.blockQualityGoodDesc: Has clear logic, assumptions, and limitations.
contentSlides.blockQualityC4: Multiple methods – not just one approach
contentSlides.blockQualityRq2: Is it clear where the model infers vs. relies on facts?
contentSlides.blockQualityRq3: Is the decision logic shown, not just the conclusion?
contentSlides.blockQualityRq5: Are there alternatives or opposing hypotheses?
contentSlides.blockQualityRed1: Answer too fast and too certain
contentSlides.blockQualityInlineIntro: Before giving the final answer, AI must check:
contentSlides.blockQualityInlineTemplate:
Before giving the final answer:
- check logical consistency (do all claims align?)
- state assumptions (which facts rely on guesswork, not data?)
- note uncertainties (where is data missing or risky?)
- correct factual guesses (are all numbers and facts accurate?)
contentSlides.blockQualityInlineExample: "Before giving Q4 strategy recommendations, check: do all KPIs align with budget? State which forecasts rely on assumptions."
contentSlides.blockQualityPostHocIntro: After the first answer, ask AI to review:
contentSlides.blockQualityPostHocTemplate:
Review the previous answer and list:
1) Logical gaps (where is logic incomplete or contradictory?)
2) Unsupported claims (where is data or sources missing?)
3) Assumptions (which facts rely on guesswork, not evidence?)
4) Improvement suggestions (what can be improved or added?)
contentSlides.blockQualityPostHocExample: "Review the Q4 sales analysis. Is the comparison with Q3 complete? What assumptions about Q1 growth?"
contentSlides.blockQualitySafetyBelt: Quality Control = safety belt: compensates for weak context (Input), unclear goal (Meta), and an overconfident model (Advanced).
contentSlides.blockFullExampleHero3: Real business example – copy it, adapt to your task.
contentSlides.blockFullExampleMeta: You are a senior business strategist with 12 years of B2B SaaS experience. Your goal is to prepare a Q4 sales strategy presentation for board members who will make strategic decisions for 2025.
contentSlides.blockFullExampleInput: Q1–Q3 2024: 2.1M EUR (+22% vs 2023), 156 new clients, average deal 13.5k EUR. Competitors: Competitor A (+18%), Competitor B (+15%). Q4 budget: 500k EUR.
contentSlides.blockFullExampleOutput: 10 slides: Executive Summary, Current situation, Competitor analysis, Q4 goals, Strategy, Action plan, Budget, Risks, Metrics, Conclusions. Format: PowerPoint, business tone.
contentSlides.blockFullExampleReasoning: 1) Define the key message 2) Analyse data 3) Evaluate options 4) Compare with competitors 5) Identify trade-offs 6) Recommend strategy
contentSlides.blockFullExampleQuality: ✓ Clear message ✓ Data-backed ✓ Realistic ROI ✓ Action plan ✓ Risk analysis ✓ Measurable goals
contentSlides.blockFullExampleAdvanced: Temperature: 0.4. Reasoning: Deep. Max tokens: [150–300]. Top-p: [0.5–0.8]. Frequency penalty: [0.3–0.5]. Presence penalty: [0.0–0.3]. Language: EN. Format: Business document.
contentSlides.blockFullExampleTemplate:
META: You are [role with experience]. Your goal is [outcome] for [audience], who [context].

INPUT: [Concrete data, numbers, metrics]. [Constraints, context, background].

OUTPUT: [Format and structure]. [Language, tone, style].

REASONING: 1) [First step] 2) [Second step] 3) [Third step] 4) [Fourth step] 5) [Fifth step] 6) [Sixth step].

QUALITY: ✓ [Criterion 1] ✓ [Criterion 2] ✓ [Criterion 3] ✓ [Criterion 4] ✓ [Criterion 5].

ADVANCED: Temperature: [0.2–0.7]. Reasoning: [normal/extended]. Max tokens: [150–300]. Top-p: [0.5–0.8]. Frequency penalty: [0.3–0.5]. Presence penalty: [0.0–0.3]. Language: [EN]. Format: [Business document/Presentation/Article].
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2Goal: Goal: control response length, focus, and repetition without extra "prompt noise".
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2Warning: Advanced parameters will never fix a badly phrased task.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2Warning2: First – a clear task. Then – parameters.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2MaxTokensControls: how much text AI can generate
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2MaxExampleTask: Answer in 3 bullet points. No examples or elaboration.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2MaxError: Error: Increasing Max Tokens instead of asking for structure or bullets.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2TopPControls: how many options AI considers before answering
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2TopPExampleTask: Give the single best solution. Don't list alternatives.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2TopPRule: Rule: High Temperature + high Top-p = low predictability
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2FreqControls: word and phrase repetition in text
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2FreqExampleTask: Write text without repeated phrases or clichés.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2PresenceControls: whether AI sticks to the topic or actively explores new ideas
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2PresenceRule: Business rule: Keep Presence penalty ≤ 0.3 for analysis
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2SafeNote: Stable, predictable, safe for internal use.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2OneRuleText: If the AI response is bad – in 90% of cases the task is at fault, not the parameters.
contentSlides.blockAdvanced2Template: ADVANCED: Max tokens: [150-300]. Top-p: [0.5-0.8]. Frequency penalty: [0.3-0.5]. Presence penalty: [0.0-0.3].
contentSlides.practiceTasksHint: All practical tasks in this training can be done with any modern generative AI tool.
contentSlides.introBodyRlOrder: AI models read and process information sequentially. The most important information should be given first so the result meets your expectations.
contentSlides.checkText: Go back and change the parameters.
contentSlides.preCopyCheckHeading: Before copying: is the brief complete?

aiDetectors.title: AI content detection tools (2026)
aiDetectors.intro: These tools help determine whether content (text, images, video) was generated by AI. Use them to assess content authenticity – not to hide AI use.
aiDetectors.tldrBody: AI detectors are for checking content authenticity, not for hiding AI use. Results are not 100% accurate – interpret critically.
aiDetectors.doNowBody1: Copy the 6-block prompt (below) into your AI tool and ask it to explain how to use a detector – the tool will suggest steps.
aiDetectors.doNowBody2: Pick a detector from the table below – e.g. Isgen.ai (LT) for Lithuanian text.
aiDetectors.howToTitle: How to use a detector (enter text)
aiDetectors.howToStep2: Paste the text you want to check.
aiDetectors.howToStep3: Run the analysis (e.g. “Check” or similar button).
aiDetectors.howToStep4: Interpret the result – percentages and labels show likelihood; interpret critically.
aiDetectors.emptyState: No tools found. Try changing the search criteria.
aiDetectors.ethicsNote: Detectors are for assessing content authenticity, not for hiding AI use. No detector is 100% accurate – interpret results critically.
